We want you to know that you don’t have to face challenges alone—we’re here for you! ❤️ Our Food Pantry is open and ready to serve our neighbors with fresh groceries and support. Check out our current hours!

我們希望讓您知道，您不必獨自面對挑戰——我們一直在這裡支持您！ ❤️ 我們的食物發放中心已準備就緒，為鄰里提供新鮮食材與關懷。請查看開放時間！

#RiverOfLifeFoundation #ROLF #FoodPantry #CommunitySupport #EndHunger WeAreHereForYou 生命河基金會 食物倉庫 社區關懷 慈善公益

Dear Volunteers, Yesterday’s Event Shone Bright Because of You 🌟 You Were the True Stars!

Those of you who started setting up the night before still arrived early in the cold yesterday;
some didn’t even get a bite to eat, some ran around covering every little need,
some comforted nervous children with your smiles, and some stayed until the very end to restore the venue.

Every small action made hundreds of families feel warmth.
Because of you, children laughed with joy, and every family could fully enjoy the happiness of Thanksgiving.

🙏 Dear volunteers, thank you for your hard work.
Thanks for all the love and effort you put into our community!
We are so proud to have all of you!

親愛的志工們，昨天的活動因你們而精彩 🌟 最亮眼的，其實是你！

從前一天晚上就開始忙碌場佈的你們，昨天依然頂著低溫提早到場；
有人忙到沒吃一口午餐、有人從頭到尾補位跑動、
有人用笑容安撫緊張的小朋友、有人留下來把場地恢復原樣。

每一個小小的動作，都讓成千上百個家庭感受到溫暖。
因為你們，孩子們開心地笑出聲，每個家庭都能盡情享受感恩節的歡樂。

🙏 親愛的志工們，辛苦了。
謝謝你們為社區付出的努力跟愛！
我們很驕傲能擁有你們！

Starting in November, if you come at the old time,
you might find the Food Pantry closed — just like in the video!
We’ll be opening earlier beginning November!

🕤 Tues / Thurs: 9:30 AM – 12:30 PM
🕚 Fri: 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM

📍Same location, just an earlier start to make things easier for everyone.
💛 See you in November!

如果 11 月開始還照以前的時間來領食物，就會像影片裡那樣——發現 Food Pantry 沒開門喔！
從 11 月起，我們會提早開門！

🕤 週二 / 週四：上午 9:30 – 下午 12:30
🕚 週五：上午 11:00 – 下午 1:00

📍地點不變，只是時間更早，讓大家更方便前來。
💛 我們 11 月見！

🎉 180 Laptop Refurbishing Officially Completed! 🎉

Take a look at these amazing faces! 📸 These incredible young volunteers poured their time, energy, and kind hearts into a massive mission: refurbishing 180 Chromebooks for our community! 💻✨
Because of their hard work, these laptops are ready to be distributed to children from low-income families, giving them a much better opportunity to study, learn, and explore the digital world. 🚀📚
A huge thank you to these wonderful kids for their dedication. Our community is growing stronger and brighter every day because of your incredible contribution! 🌟❤️

#ROLF #CommunityFirst #YouthVolunteers #GivingBack #TechForGood #EducationMatters #Inspiration

Community in Action! 💙🍎

On March 5th, we were thrilled to host a group of volunteers from our local Fremont Whole Foods at the ROLF Food Pantry. From the first crate to the last pallet, their hard work and smiles made a huge impact on our operations.

Partnerships like these are what allow us to continue our mission of providing fresh, healthy food to those in need. Thank you, Whole Foods team, for your dedication to the Fremont community!

📍 River of Life Foundation Food Pantry
🤝 Partner Spotlight: Whole Foods Market (Fremont)

Zero Waste Innovations Grant Spotlight: @MarthasKitchenSJ

Martha’s Kitchen provides nutritious meals in schools, senior centers, shelters, and more, by mobilizing volunteers and rescued food ensuring everyone gets access. Their project will expand its edible food recovery infrastructure, which will increase donated meals, reduce hunger, while preventing landfilled food waste.

Learn more about their organization and how you can get involved at Marthas-Kitchen.org.

This program is funded by the City of San José Zero Waste Innovations Grant (ZWIG), which supports creative, community-driven solutions that reduce solid waste, while promoting sustainability and a circular economy. Learn more about the #ZWIG program and our grantees at SJEnvironment.org/ZWIGrants.

A heartfelt thank you to the Intero Foundation for supporting senior meal services in San Benito County! 💙

Between July 2025 and March 2026, Martha's Kitchen delivered 20,000 meals to 150 homebound seniors, helping ensure our neighbors could age with dignity and access nutritious food from the comfort of their homes.

During that same period, we served 290 seniors and provided 10,000 congregate meals, creating opportunities for connection, community, and nourishment.

As families continue to struggle with the high cost of living, thousands of local students lack the basic school supplies they need to start the school year ready to learn.

According to the 2025 Silicon Valley Pain Index:

- More than 2,200 San José students are experiencing homelessness, nearly triple the number reported in 2020.
- Santa Clara County has the nation's highest rate of unsheltered, unaccompanied homeless youth.
- More than 26,500 children in the county live below the federal poverty level.

Each year, Sacred Heart Community Service provides backpacks and school supplies to more than 3,500 students, with more than 900 new families joining the program last year alone.

You can also be part of supporting Pack a Back 2026 by volunteering, sharing this post, donating and getting involved! 

More information in the link in our bio!

Today, Monday, January 19, Sacred Heart Community Service will be closed in observance of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. 

Our vision of a community united, where every child and adult can live free from poverty, deeply aligns with Dr. King’s lifelong commitment to racial and economic justice.

As we honor his legacy, we invite our community to reflect on his teachings and continue working toward the beloved community he envisioned. Seek out his full speeches, writings, and the history often left untold.
Reflection is not passive. It is a call to action.

Have you heard of Chefs of Compassion? ❤️🧑‍🍳 To stay up to date with Bay Area events, things to do, and food recs, follow @felicitythefoodie ! 
•
🔖 Save this for your next community event! 
•
📍 West Valley Community Services - Silicon Valley, CA 
•
As a foodie, I’m a big believer that FOOD IS LOVE. Sharing a meal with someone is one of the greatest forms of connection so it felt like a seamless fit to attend @wvcommunityservices ‘ Chefs of Compassion event and learn about their mission of combating hunger and homelessness in the West Valley area of Santa Clara County. 
•
The evening was an eye-opening reminder of real needs that often go unseen in the Bay Area. Hearing about the impact of their services firsthand inspired me to find time to volunteer at their food market, where I was able to see their dedication to providing nourishing, fresh food to the community. If it’s been a while since you’ve done some community service and/or you’re looking for ways to make change in your community, I highly recommend looking into the amazing projects they have. Thank you @wvcommunityservices !❤️

Through storytelling and hands-on service, 'Seeds of Compassion', our family volunteering program, helps instill the value of compassion in action from an early age.

On the first Saturday of June, families came together to  organize and clean our food pantry, and prepare resources for neighbors facing hardship. Alongside these meaningful volunteer activities, participants learned about the challenges many families in our community face and how even small acts of kindness can make a lasting difference.

Thank you to all the wonderful families who joined us. Your compassion, generosity, and willingness to serve are helping build a stronger, more caring community 💙

This Fourth of July, we honor those who fought for our freedoms and remember that many veterans now face food insecurity here at home.

For veterans, families, older adults, and children facing food insecurity, nutritious food shouldn’t be a privilege. It should be the most basic of rights.

At Loaves & Fishes, our goal is to ensure everyone has access to healthy meals, regardless of their circumstances. Every meal we serve is made to nourish, welcome, and remind our neighbors they are not alone.

See link in bio to donate.

organization_name: Catholic Charities of Santa Clara County
caption_text:
Storytime came to life in the most unforgettable way! 
Families at our Educare family resource center enjoyed an exciting interactive reading of We're Going on a Bear Hunt, where children stomped through the "grass," splashed through the "river," and bravely searched for the bear alongside storytellers from Bay Area Discovery Museum.
This hands-on adventure included sculpting scenes from the book made from dry clay and wildflowers. This lively experience sparked imaginations, encouraged a love of reading, and created joyful memories for children and parents alike. We're so grateful to our community partners for bringing enriching experiences like this to local families and making learning fun for everyone! Learn more about our Family Resource Centers:  https://bit.ly/CCSCC-FRC 
FIRST 5 Santa Clara County San Jose Public Library Franklin-McKinley School District 
#CCSCC #museum

organization_name: Catholic Charities of Santa Clara County
caption_text:
Handicapables is a warm, joy-first program for adults and older adults with disabilities that brings people together for conversation, music, shared meals, guest speakers, gentle activities, and outings — all with accessibility and companionship at the heart. It’s less about a schedule and more about finding a place to laugh, learn, and belong.

Next event dates: 
• July 18, 2026 
• August 1, 2026 
• August 8, 2026

If you’d like to join, please confirm your presence and get full event details from Saida — she’ll share the agenda, accessibility supports, and any accommodations available.

Why come?
• Make friends in a relaxed, welcoming space
• Try new things — speakers, music, outings, and activities
• Get support — accessibility and accommodations are prioritized

RSVP & details
Saida Nagpurwala 
Phone: (408) 325‑5257
Email: snagpurwala@catholiccharitiesscc.org

Learn more: https://bit.ly/handicapables

#Handicapables #CommunityBelongs #AccessibleEvents #InclusionMatters #RSVP

organization_name: Catholic Charities of Santa Clara County
caption_text:
🏀 Late Night Gym: Expanding Safe, Supportive Spaces for Youth – Now in 3 Locations! 🌟
We are thrilled to announce that Late Night Gym is expanding its reach to provide even more safe, supportive, and fun spaces for our youth. We are now operating in San Jose, Morgan Hill, and Gilroy every single week to meet the growing needs of our community!

Free and structured for 6th to 12th graders, the program reduces unsupervised time and creates consistent environments where youth can thrive.

By integrating physical activity with academic assistance and mentorship from @Catholic Charities of Santa Clara County (CCSCC) staff, the program strengthens engagement and builds healthy peer relationships.

📍 Where & When to Find Us:
San Jose
📍 Washington United Youth Center
921 S. First Street, Suite B
San Jose, CA 95110
🗓️ Tuesdays & Thursdays
⏰ 4:00 PM – 8:00 PM

Morgan Hill
📍 Centennial Recreation Center (CRC)
171 W Edmundson Ave.
Morgan Hill, CA 95037
🗓️ Every Saturday
⏰ 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM

Gilroy
📍 Wheeler Center
270 W 6th St.
Gilroy, CA 95020
🗓️ Every Thursday
⏰ 5:30 PM – 8:30 PM

🎮 What’s Happening?
Enjoy a variety of supervised activities, including:

Basketball & Recreational Sports

Video Games & Board Games

Homework Help & Academic Support

"Activity of the Day!"

Team-building & Mentorship

📞 Questions?
San Jose: Contact Jessyca Montalvo at (408) 938-6731 Ext 802

Morgan Hill & Gilroy:

Martha Diaz: (408) 673-4800 or mdiaz3@ccscc.org

Esteban Escalante: (408) 483-4801 or eescalante@ccscc.org

Join us in fostering resilience and success for our youth! 🌟
#LateNightGym #SafeSpaces #YouthDevelopment #SanJose #MorganHill #Gilroy #CatholicCharitiesSCC #CommunityConnectedness

East San José deserves equitable investment and real economic opportunity. Today, we called on the City to fund the Economic Revitalization Framework so our small businesses, workers, and families can thrive. This plan aligns with the City Council–approved two-year Economic Strategy and will guide investment, strengthen local businesses, and leverage cultural assets for long-term growth. Let’s invest in jobs, culture, and opportunity for our community.
———
East San José merece una inversión equitativa y una oportunidad económica real. Hoy, hemos pedido a la ciudad que financie el Marco de Revitalización Económica para que nuestras pequeñas empresas, trabajadores y familias puedan prosperar. Este plan se alinea con la Estrategia Económica de dos años aprobada por el Ayuntamiento y guiará la inversión, fortalecerá a los negocios locales y aprovechará los activos culturales para el crecimiento a largo plazo. Invirtamos en empleos, cultura y oportunidades para nuestra comunidad.

Every Community Day Begins with Possibility

Community Day is the heart of Sunday Friends. Held twice each month, Community Day brings together approximately 150 families (over 1,000 each year) who are working toward their individual and family goals. Throughout the day, children participate in educational activities and adults engage in workshops, training, and other things designed to build long-term stability.

This image captures a quiet moment just before the program begins at 11 AM, a reflection of the teamwork, preparation, and community commitment that make Community Day possible.

A heartfelt thank you to Sunday Friends volunteers and our staff team whose dedication makes Community Days possible. Visit our website to learn how you can volunteer, contribute, or support programs that create lasting impact.

🚲 Thaco Thursday Tours #3: "Tour de Hoover!" with our friends at @Western Wheelers Bicycle Club

This month's TTT takes us through Stanford's most iconic "Hoover-centric" landmarks — a school, a park, a historic home, a memorial plaza, a medical campus, and the crown jewel: Hoover Tower itself! 🗼
This shorter-than-usual route gives us ample time to soak in the lofty Hoover Tower vistas, and enjoy delicious tacos and more at La Cazuela, timed close to the lunch hour! 🌮
📍 Group size is capped at 20 due to Hoover Tower visitor requirements, so register now before all of the spaces are gone:
👉 https://bit.ly/svbe-ttt-july16 (or go to the svbe.org website, volunteer/calendar and register from the link shown on July 16)

📋 Important Info:
• Hoover Tower entry fee is $8/person (no cash — card only)
• Bring a lock to secure bikes at the racks near the front entrance
• Wear or pack walkable shoes — hard floors up top!
• We'll spend 40+ minutes at the tower soaking in the unique 360° views from this 285 ft, 85-year-old icon

🕘 Ride Details:
• All TTT rides start from SVBE: arrive 9:45am, roll out 10am
• 20 miles, mellow 340 ft of gain
• Hoover Tower stop at mile 10.2
• • Lunch at La Cazuela, timed near the lunch hour
Snack Stop at @patagoniapaloalto, mile 12
• Social pace, quiet residential roads & paths — no one gets dropped!

✅ Reminders:
• Check that your tires & brakes are trustworthy
• Helmets REQUIRED
• Nice weather expected — mid-to-high 70s near the bay! Bring a water bottle and wear light/bright colors to stay visible 🌞

See you Thursday! 🚴‍♀️🚴‍♂️

(Hoover Tower photo attribution: King of Hearts / Wikimedia Commons / CC-BY-SA-3.0)

🚲 Thaco Thursday Tours #3: "Tour de Hoover!" with our friends at @Western Wheelers Bicycle Club

This month's TTT takes us through Stanford's most iconic "Hoover-centric" landmarks — a school, a park, a historic home, a memorial plaza, a medical campus, and the crown jewel: Hoover Tower itself! 🗼
This shorter-than-usual route gives us ample time to soak in the lofty Hoover Tower vistas, and enjoy delicious tacos and more at La Cazuela, timed close to the lunch hour! 🌮
📍 Group size is capped at 20 due to Hoover Tower visitor requirements, so register now before all of the spaces are gone:
👉 https://bit.ly/svbe-ttt-july16 (or go to the svbe.org website, volunteer/calendar and register from the link shown on July 16)

📋 Important Info:
• Hoover Tower entry fee is $8/person (no cash — card only)
• Bring a lock to secure bikes at the racks near the front entrance
• Wear or pack walkable shoes — hard floors up top!
• We'll spend 40+ minutes at the tower soaking in the unique 360° views from this 285 ft, 85-year-old icon

🕘 Ride Details:
• All TTT rides start from SVBE: arrive 9:45am, roll out 10am
• 20 miles, mellow 340 ft of gain
• Hoover Tower stop at mile 10.2
• • Lunch at La Cazuela, timed near the lunch hour
Snack Stop at @patagoniapaloalto, mile 12
• Social pace, quiet residential roads & paths — no one gets dropped!

✅ Reminders:
• Check that your tires & brakes are trustworthy
• Helmets REQUIRED
• Nice weather expected — mid-to-high 70s near the bay! Bring a water bottle and wear light/bright colors to stay visible 🌞

See you Thursday! 🚴‍♀️🚴‍♂️

(Hoover Tower photo attribution: King of Hearts / Wikimedia Commons / CC-BY-SA-3.0)
